title: Oreogrammitis merrillii
intro: Oreogrammitis merrillii, also known as Merrill's fern or Japanese cliff fern, is a species of epiphytic fern native to Southeast Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties, which may aid in the treatment of respiratory issues, wounds, and other infections. The bioactive constituents of Oreogrammitis merrillii include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Oreogrammitis merrillii is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including fever, coughs, and skin irritations.
taxonomy: Oreogrammitis merrillii is a species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae. It is native to the Philippines and is classified within the genus Oreogrammitis. The genus Oreogrammitis belongs to the order Polypodiales and the class Polypodiopsida. This classification places Oreogrammitis merrillii within the group of vascular plants known as ferns. Further classification also groups it with other species of ferns within the kingdom Plantae.
uses: Oreogrammitis merrillii has been traditionally used in some regions for its medicinal properties.
